summary: Tuesday listings emphasized wounded-parton multiplicity scaling across light and heavy ions, GMC-mitigated rapidity-even directed flow, C_pT baselines using finite-muB lattice-QCD equation-of-state inputs, formal relativistic-hydrodynamic limits from spectral bounds and shock regularity, dense-matter trace-anomaly channel diagnostics, and HEP AI/ML benchmarks for event classification and calorimeter fast simulation.
strongest_paper: 2607.16485 is the strongest directly heavy-ion contribution: it tests one wounded-parton Glauber plus fluctuation framework across O+O, Ne+Ne, Xe+Xe, and Pb+Pb multiplicity distributions with common parameters.

papers.title: Lorentz-Covariant Spectral Bounds from Thermal Quantum Field Theory
papers.authors: Alisher Sanetullaev, Sarbinaz Bazarbaeva, Marhabo Beymamatova, Shokir Tursunov
papers.abstract: Derives Lorentz-covariant bounds on relaxation spectra and hydrodynamic gradient-expansion radii from retarded Green's functions, with holographic quasinormal-mode checks.

papers.title: Breakdown of Smooth Shock Solutions in Transient Relativistic Hydrodynamics
papers.authors: Davi D. de Oliveira, Gabriel S. Denicol
papers.abstract: Shows that Israel-Stewart shock solutions lose regularity above a critical velocity, producing a subshock, and tests regularization procedures.

papers.title: Trace anomaly and isospin splitting in inverse-mapped relativistic mean-field theory
papers.abstract: Uses an inverse-mapped RMF ensemble constrained by chiral EFT, heavy-ion flow, and neutron-star data to analyze trace-anomaly and sound-speed splittings as probes of high-density isovector response.

papers.title: Wounded parton scaling of multiplicities in ultra-relativistic light- and heavy-ion collisions
papers.authors: Rupam Samanta, Piotr Bozek, Wojciech Broniowski
papers.abstract: Fits charged-particle multiplicity distributions in O+O, Ne+Ne, Xe+Xe, and Pb+Pb at around 5 TeV using a common wounded-parton Glauber model with negative-binomial fluctuations.

papers.title: Rapidity-even Dipolar Flow in Relativistic Heavy-Ion Collisions
papers.abstract: Studies v1-even and mixed-harmonic correlations in Au+Au with AMPT and HIJING while suppressing leading global-momentum-conservation effects using eta-dependent weights.

papers.title: Sensitivity of p_T Fluctuations to the QCD Equation of State
papers.authors: Rutik Manikandhan, Rohit Gupta, Rene Bellwied
papers.abstract: Develops a C_pT baseline across RHIC BES energies using finite-muB lattice-QCD EoS inputs and a minimalist fluid-dynamic evolution.

papers.title: Benchmarking Machine Learning Architectures for ttH Multilepton Signal Sensitivity
papers.authors: Lukas Vicenik, Andre Sopczak, Oleksandr Shekhovtsov
papers.abstract: Compares XGBoost, LorentzNet, Particle Transformer, and related architectures on synthetic ttH multilepton signal-background classification with standardized training and sensitivity metrics.

papers.title: CaloTrilogy: Toward a Breakthrough in One-Step, End-to-End, Physics-Guided Shower Generation for Modern Calorimeters
papers.authors: Cheng Jiang, Sitian Qian, Kevin Pedro, Oz Amram, Huilin Qu, Maggie Voetberg
papers.abstract: Combines an average-velocity-field integrator, learned shower-space prior, and physics-guided losses for one-step or few-step high-granularity calorimeter shower generation.

papers.title: Chemonuclear Transmutation and Noble Metal Synthesis
papers.abstract: Claims enormous low-temperature fusion enhancement, radioactive-waste transmutation, and noble-metal synthesis in hydride-electron donor mixtures.
